enrichment.field: computational catalysis (MLIP / DFT energetics, MAE in eV)
enrichment.policy: deterministic verdicts kept verbatim; bare planner abstains turned into reasoned CONFIRMED-CORRECT review notes, ADVISORY routes, or SUBJECTIVE routes; only genuinely unassessable claims remain abstained

abstained_kept.c13: ambiguous baseline for 'Er(MO) decreases by 0.07 eV' (0.07 only matches FT-BMA+MD(M)->FT-All; FT-MD(MO)->FT-All is 0.029) -> abstain>guess (DESIGN 3.4)
abstained_kept.c37: MD settings 10 ps / NVT / 300 K are descriptive method params (tier null); nothing to recompute

findings.message: reported total equals the sum of its parts (within printing precision)
reported total equals the sum of its parts (within printing precision)
reported total equals the sum of its parts (within printing precision)

routed_to_human.note: Headline '>2x error reduction from 5-10% perturbed configs' is an over-generalization of the table. The 2x holds for one specific pair (FS-All Er(M) 0.279 vs FS-BMA 0.659 = 2.36x), but FS-BMA already contains MD/perturbed data and the per-config-fraction framing is not isolated in any single comparison. What would confirm: an ablation table holding everything fixed except the 5-10% perturbed fraction, with the resulting Er/Ea ratio reported.
routed_to_human.quote:
that FS models with 5 % to 10 % of perturbed (high-energy) configurations from
MD or contour exploring (CE), can reduce the error by over 2× compared to
models trained only on relaxation trajectories.

routed_to_human.note: Abstract OER deltas don't reconcile with the body. Abstract rounds FT-All OER to 0.30 eV and states it is 0.08 eV below MACE-MH-1 and 0.14 eV below the best FS MLIP; but body values (FT-All 0.278, MACE-MH-1 0.384, FS-All 0.438 eV over 96 reactions) give deltas of 0.106 and 0.160 eV. Sign and ranking are right but the stated gaps are understated by ~0.02-0.03 eV. What would confirm: state the abstract deltas from the un-rounded 0.278 eV figure, or footnote which MAE the 0.30 eV refers to.
routed_to_human.quote:
an MLIP fine-tuned on metallic catalysts achieves an MAE
of 0.30 eV for OER on iridium oxide polymorphs, which is 0.08 eV lower than
out-of-box MACE-MH-1 and 0.14 eV lower than the best performing FS MLIP.

routed_to_human.note: Prose-vs-table sign error. Text says FT-BMA Er(MO)=0.334 eV is 'about 0.06 eV lower' than the ~2,692-MO-config model (FT-MD(MO)=0.306 eV), but 0.334 is 0.028 eV HIGHER, not lower. Either the direction word or the 0.06 magnitude is wrong. What would confirm: a corrected sentence keyed to the Table 5 entries (0.334 vs 0.306).
routed_to_human.quote:
with
no metal oxide in the training set, the Er(MO) MAE is 0.334 eV, about 0.06 eV lower
than a model that had about 2,692 metal-oxides configurations in the training set.

routed_to_human.note: Causal mechanistic attribution carries no evidence ref. The claim that the FS-MD model's ~0.2 eV barrier error 'arises because it predicted a different initial state, not the transition state' is a plausible computational-catalysis failure mode, but it is asserted without the per-image energy decomposition that would isolate initial- vs transition-state error. What would confirm: side-by-side DFT-vs-MLIP energies for the NEB initial, transition, and final images for that reaction.
routed_to_human.quote:
the 0.2 eV higher
error in predicting the barrier by the FS-MD model arises because it has predicted
a different initial state as compared to DFT, not the transition state.

routed_to_human.note: Over-stated range. Conclusion says all fine-tuned MLIPs have NEB Ea MAE 'between 0.14 to 0.15 eV', but Table 6 (S7) lists FT-MD(M)=0.159 eV and FT-BMA+MD(M)=0.153 eV, both above 0.15 eV. The 'best FS' 0.175 eV comparator is fine. What would confirm: widen the stated range to ~0.14-0.16 eV or restrict the 'all' to the models that actually fall in [0.14,0.15].
routed_to_human.quote:
all our
MLIPs fine-tuned to a different training set each have a MAE of between 0.14 to
0.15 eV, better than our best performing from scratch model (MAE = 0.175 eV).

routed_to_human.note: Whether the 49,860-config fine-tuned MLIP 'shows the best performance across both metallic and metal-oxide catalysts' and is the practically meaningful approach for large alloy screening is an importance/significance judgement (T8), not a numeric check. The supporting number is faithful (Er MAE 0.15 eV on the 2,115-config DFT subset of the 90,781-structure screen matches Table-derived values), but whether that accuracy is sufficient for the intended discovery use is for a domain reader to weigh.
routed_to_human.quote:
a large MLIP fine-tuned to 49,860
configurations shows the best performance across both metallic and metal-oxide
catalysts, which was used to screen a large-left out, 90,781, of bimetallic alloys,
achieving an MAE of 0.15 eV for Er

routed_to_human.note: Abstract Ea MAE 0.19 eV for OOD CO2RR on Cu rounds faithfully from the FT-MD(MO) Ea(M)=0.184 eV body/Table-5 value; no over-reach. No numeric prose-vs-table verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
an MLIP fine-tuned to O and OH adsorption on metal oxides gives
an Ea MAE of 0.19 eV for out-of-distribution CO2RR reaction on Cu

routed_to_human.note: FS-BMA Er(M)=0.659 eV transcribes Table 4 exactly; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers this figure-read MAE yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
achieves
an MAE of 0.659 eV when predicting the metal test set Er

routed_to_human.note: FS-BMA+MD Er(M)=0.310 / Er(MO)=0.476 eV transcribe Table 4 exactly; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the
model achieves an MAE of 0.310 eV for metallic catalyst Er, better than the performance of the unmodified large foundation models (UMA and MACE-MH-1), but
performs poorly for Er(MO) with an MAE of 0.476 eV

routed_to_human.note: FS-All Ea(M)=0.175 eV matches Table 4; '~about half' of FS-BMA+MD (0.398 eV) checks out (ratio 0.44). Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
This model achieves an MAE
of 0.175 eV for Ea(M) predictions, about half of the error calculated using the
FS-BMA+MD model, within 0.05 eV of the UMA and MACE-MH-1 models’ MAE.

routed_to_human.note: FT-MD(M)−FT-BMA Er(M) = 0.376−0.238 = 0.138 eV, rounds to the stated 0.15 eV increase (within figure-read/rounding tolerance). Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
The FT-MD(M) model performs worse than the FT-BMA model
with a 0.15 eV increase in Er(M) MAE.

routed_to_human.note: FT-MD(M) Er(MO)=0.428 / FT-BMA Er(MO)=0.334 eV transcribe Table 5 exactly; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the FT-MD(M) model MAE for Er(MO) is 0.428 eV
while the FT-BMA MAE is 0.334 eV

routed_to_human.note: FT-CE Er(MO)=0.305 vs FT-MD(MO)=0.306 eV transcribe Table 5 exactly ('almost identical' is apt); reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
performs better for Er(MO) (MAE = 0.305 eV) than
the FT-BMA and FT-MD(M) models. It also has almost identical performance to the
FT-MD(MO) model (MAE = 0.306 eV)

routed_to_human.note: CHCOH* pathway MAEs (MH-1 1.011, FS-BMA 0.455, FS-All 0.251, FT-All 0.141 eV) read off Fig 3a; ordering and values internally consistent, no over-reach. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
MACE-MH-1 (OMAT) performs the worst with an MAE of 1.011 eV. The
FS-BMA model exhibits an error of of up to 0.5 eV for each elementary step (overall
MAE of 0.455 eV)

routed_to_human.note: FT-All MAE 0.141 eV (7 rxns) / 0.143 eV (101 rxns) read off figures; consistent and not over-stated. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the best performing model
with an MAE of 0.141 eV across these 7 reactions, and similarly achieve an MAE of
0.143 eV across the entire 101 reactions reported across other catalysts in this study.

routed_to_human.note: FT-All C1-pathway MAE 0.124 eV vs DFT read off Fig 3b; reviewed, no over-reach. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
The FT-All model
(solid lines) predicts the energy of each step with an MAE of 0.124 eV with respect to
the DFT values

routed_to_human.note: Count of 23 DFT NEB reactions from Ref [62] matches Table 6 (n_reactions=23); reviewed, faithful transcription. No verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Their work provides 23 DFT NEB
reactions created using similar DFT settings as those used to train our MLIPs

routed_to_human.note: FT-All single-point-on-NEB-final-images barrier difference of 0.3 eV transcribes the S6 figure-read value; reviewed, no over-reach. No verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
observed a difference of 0.3 eV
in barrier predictions, indicating a high sensitivity of Ea to the structures at the initial
and transitions states.

routed_to_human.note: Count of 96 OER reactions from Ref [58] is a faithful citation transcription; reviewed, no over-reach. No verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
calculated the OER energetics for a total of 96 reactions on rutile (R), α,
and β phases of several different surface terminations of IrO2 and IrO3 under applied
potential.

routed_to_human.note: FS-All OER: 0.21 eV (O/OH), 1.6 eV (OOH*), overall 0.438 eV across 96 reactions transcribe §4.2.2 faithfully; the OOH* outlier is openly attributed to OOH* absent from training, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
has an error of 0.21 eV for O and OH adsorption. However,
the error is significantly higher for OOH* adsorption at 1.6 eV, which is attributed
to the absence of OOH* species in the training set. For the 96 reactions reported in
Ref. [58], the FS-All model has an MAE of 0.438 eV.

routed_to_human.note: MACE-MH-1 OER MAE 0.384 eV and the 0.05 eV gap vs FS-All (0.438) check out (0.438−0.384=0.054, rounds to 0.05). Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
reduces
the error for OOH* to less than 0.5 eV, and has an over-
all MAE of 0.384 eV, which is a 0.05 eV lower than FS-All model.

routed_to_human.note: FT-MD(MO)=0.313 / FT-All=0.278 eV OER MAEs (96 rxns) transcribe §4.2.2 exactly; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
The total MAEs for the
96 reaction for FT-MD(MO) and FT-All models are 0.313 and 0.278 eV, respectively,
both showing better performance than the from-scratch models.

routed_to_human.note: Screening counts 90,781 initialized / 90,181 converged are descriptive totals stated consistently; reviewed, no over-reach. (Not a sum-decomposition, so sum_check does not apply.)
routed_to_human.quote:
This procedure produced 90,781 total initialized structures,
of which 90,181 converged.

routed_to_human.note: FT-All adsorption-energy MAE 0.15 eV on the 2,115-config DFT subset transcribes §4.2.3 exactly; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: For these 2,115 configurations, the FT-All model adsorption energy MAE is 0.15 eV

routed_to_human.note: Per-termination %-within-±0.2 eV (311:77, 532:58, others 65–75) read off Fig 6a; internally consistent, no over-reach. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
For the (311) surface termination, 77% of configurations are within the target
threshold of ±0.2 eV. The accuracy is lowest for the (532) facet, the highest surface
termination we considered, with only 58% of predictions within the error threshold.
Nevertheless for all other surface terminations, 65% to 75% of the tested configurations
lie within with the ±0.2 eV tolerance.

routed_to_human.note: Per-composition %-within-±0.2 eV (AgNi 68, CuAu 46, CuIr 73) read off Fig 6b; consistent, no over-reach. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
For AgNi and CuAu, the performance is mediocre
with only 68% and 46% within ±0.2 eV of the DFT values, respectively. On the other
hand, the model performance is much better for CuIr, with 73% within the threshold.

routed_to_human.note: Conclusion OER MAEs (FT-All 0.278 vs FS 0.384 eV) match the §4.2.2 body values; reviewed, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
FT-All model has an MAE
of 0.278 eV, noticeably better the error of from-scratch model (MAE = 0.384 eV).

routed_to_human.note: Conclusion '81% within ±0.2 eV' matches the §4.2.3 screening value (ev_screen pct=81); faithful, no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
with 81% of randomly selected subset of reactions having
less than ±0.2 eV error with respect to the DFT values.

routed_to_human.note: Conclusion CHCOH* MAEs (FT-All 0.141 vs FS-All 0.251 eV) match §4.2.1 / Fig 3a; reviewed, no over-reach. No figure-read verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the FT-All model achieves an MAE of 0.141 eV, noticeably better than our
best from scratch model (FS-All MAE = 0.251 eV).

routed_to_human.note: External-literature transcription (Wood et al. UMA: 74% of AdsorbML Er within 0.1 eV); a T5 citation value not recomputable from this paper. Reviewed, no distortion of the cited source evident.

is able to estimate Er within 0.1 eV of DFT, for 74
% of Er reported in AdsorbML task.

routed_to_human.note: External-literature transcription (Batatia et al.: S24 0.095 eV, OC20 0.138 eV); a T5 citation value not recomputable from this paper. Reviewed, no distortion evident.
routed_to_human.quote:
with an MAE of 0.095 eV for the S24 [25] and 0.138 eV for OC20
benchmark tests.

routed_to_human.note: External-literature transcription (CatTSunami: 56% of 900, improving to 86% with single-point DFT); a T5 citation value not recomputable here. Reviewed, no distortion evident.
routed_to_human.quote:
They found that the MLIP-
predicted NEBs were successful for 56% of the 900 reactions studied, which improved
to 86% when the ∆Ea were calculated using single-point DFT energies at the MLIP’s
initial, transition, and final state structures.

routed_to_human.note: External-literature transcription (Schaaf et al.: Ea MAE 0.05 eV, In2O3, six AL loops); a T5 citation value not recomputable here. Reviewed, no distortion evident.
routed_to_human.quote:
trained an accurate MLIP (MAE of 0.05 eV
for Ea) for CO2 hydrogenation to methanol on In2O3 in six active-learning loops
